title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: David Thomas & Andrew Hunt
notes: A foundational read. The advice on orthogonality, DRY, and tracer bullets still holds up decades later.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
notes: Thought-provoking take on startups and monopoly theory. Best read as a set of contrarian questions to ask yourself.

title: The Rust Programming Language
author: Steve Klabnik & Carol Nichols
notes: The best language book I've read. Ownership and borrowing finally clicked here.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
notes: Essential for anyone building systems at scale. The chapters on replication and consensus are worth reading twice.

title: Flash Boys
author: Michael Lewis
notes: A great primer on HFT and market microstructure, told as a thriller. Directly relevant to thinking about on-chain MEV.

title: The Innovator's Dilemma
author: Clayton Christensen
notes: Explains why good companies fail. The disruption framework is a useful lens for evaluating crypto vs. tradfi.
